banana: Curved, elongated shape with tapered ends
Distinct peel texture (often yellow or green)
Natural tapering from stem end to tip
box: Flat planes forming six sides (top, bottom, four sides)
Often sealed or closed with minimal openings
May have handles, labels, or flaps as features
camel: Large, humped body with long neck and legs
Arched back with one or two humps
Thick, shaggy coat covering most of the body
Four sturdy legs supporting a bulky torso
can: Cylindrical shape with flat circular top and bottom
Label or printed branding on side
Slight taper or ridge near top for opening
dish: Circular or oval shallow bowl shape
Smooth, concave interior for holding food
Raised rim around edge for containment
Often made of ceramic or glass with glossy finish
drill machine: Cylindrical main body with handle and trigger
Chuck at front for holding drill bits
Base or stand for stability during use
mouse: Ergonomic curved shape designed for hand grip
Smooth surface with buttons or scroll wheel
Typically has left/right click and scroll functions
pears: Oval shape with pointed tip and wider base
Smooth skin with slight dimpling or texture
Color gradient from green to yellow or red
Single stem at top connecting to fruit body
Asymmetric form due to natural growth pattern
scissors: Two blades joined at pivot point
Blades converge to sharp cutting edge
Metallic material with possible rubberized grips
shampoo bottle: Base with slight weight distribution for stability
